probe: teacher-forced byte cross-entropy of the frozen core on GOLD TEXT (no audio)
why: separates H-prior (core is Sanskrit-only) from H-capacity (adapter too small)

verdict.reading: A large positive gap means the frozen core assigns English text far lower probability than Sanskrit text — LoRA must supply the English language model itself, on top of audio conditioning. That is H-prior: an architectural ceiling of a frozen monolingual core, not an adapter-capacity shortfall.
